qa.user_verbatim_complaint: /dev 全修 — fix every bug found by the prior Codex audit relating to the /do and /allow slash-command bypass coverage across the pretool hooks. The user's verbatim grievance was that /do failed to unlock Read on large files even though /do claims to grant the main agent subagent-class permissions; they had to delegate to a subagent.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: Codex flagged that the .claude/.hook-refactor-allow sentinel (created May 19 by dev to permit hook edits, per pretool-claude-config-guard.py contract) remains on disk. Dispatch explicitly excludes this from QA failure scope ('orchestrator handles cleanup post-QA, do not flag as defect'). Dev pre-recorded the cleanup REQUEST in blocking_issues[0].

Codex also flagged a second sentinel at top-level /.hook-refactor-allow. Filesystem mtime shows April 27 — pre-dates this cycle by ~3 weeks. Not introduced by this cycle's diff. Out of cycle scope; surfaced for orchestrator/user awareness.

Codex flagged that CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME=EnterWorktree + empty stdin yields exit 0 (the hook now requires stdin JSON instead of falling back to CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME env). Independently verified: identical behavior exists in sibling hooks pretool-write-guard.sh (RC=0 on empty stdin) and pretool-bash-safety.sh (RC=0 on empty stdin) — this is the canonical fail-open-on-missing-stdin idiom for PreToolUse bash hooks in this directory. BA spec explicitly documented this in Risk 3 with the exact mitigation: 'All Claude Code PreToolUse hooks receive JSON on stdin per the canonical protocol; the existing env var is a Claude-Code-supplied convenience, not the primary contract... We additionally fall back fail-open on parse failure (same pattern as the other bash hooks).' Claude Code's actual production PreToolUse invocation sends JSON on stdin; the empty-stdin path is synthetic and does not occur in runtime. Classified as nitpick → reject (Calling Protocol Rule 3).

qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: Main-agent Read of a 700-line file with /do flag content='true' now exits 0 (was exit 2 per user's verbatim 'do还是出问题'). Main-agent Read with /allow Read grant body {'pattern':'Read','is_regex':false} also exits 0. EnterWorktree carve-out (per CLAUDE.md permanent-block list specifying only EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode) honored via /do flag (exit 0) and /allow EnterWorktree grant (exit 0). Plain main-agent operations without bypass still block with the original safety message and exit 2 (safety contract intact, AC4 + AC8). Subagent agent_id Read exemption preserved unchanged (AC3 / M6 no-regression). LINE_LIMIT restored to 600 to align with docstring + CLAUDE.md whitelist (AC5).

complaint_element_pointer.selector: main() function in /root/.claude/hooks/pretool-read-size-guard.py line 58-90 (handler for main-agent Read >LINE_LIMIT); plus full body of /root/.claude/hooks/pretool-block-enterworktree.sh (EnterWorktree handler)
complaint_element_pointer.how_located: BA spec explicit file enumeration: ticket-20260519-151734.md lines 209-211 (Affected files) — pretool-read-size-guard.py and pretool-block-enterworktree.sh

qa.overall_assessment: All 9 acceptance criteria PASS via independent smoke tests with crafted stdin JSON payloads. Additional verifications (subagent EnterWorktree still blocked, malformed-JSON fail-open, syntax checks, import audit, has_consent flag content discipline, bounded chunk + binary + non-Read still exempt) all PASS. Codex consultation surfaced two findings, both filtered as out-of-scope or nitpick per Calling Protocol Rule 3 (caller filters codex output, codex does not set verdict). User-stated complaint /do还是出问题 is directly resolved by the patch.

qa._success_criteria_rule: BA's requirements_decomposition has 1 user-need item (R1). The cycle has 9 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC9) decomposing R1 into testable conditions. All 9 verified above.

root_cause_verification.rationale: The Codex audit's root-cause finding was bypass-coverage gap (the /do flag and /allow grant were never read by the two named hooks). Both hooks now read /do flag (content equality discipline matching pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 canonical) AND call lib.allowlist.read_grant() for the matching tool. Both bypass paths verified empirically to produce exit 0. Plain (non-bypass) operations still block with verbatim safety messages. LINE_LIMIT drift introduced by commit 9204c25 reverted (1000 → 600). Novelty check (BA spec §Prior Attempts): prior attempts were L3 threshold tweaks; this fix is L4 logic adding actual bypass branches at the right layer.

build_verification.deploy_command: n/a (hooks are sourced live by Claude Code via settings.json registration; no rebuild step)
build_verification.build_output_summary: py_compile exit 0; bash -n exit 0. Both files syntactically valid.

regression_test_results.details: agent_id='subagent-abc' + 1100-line file + no /do + no /allow → exit 0 (is_exempt at line 36-37 unchanged)
tool_input.limit=100 on 700-line file → exit 0 (is_bounded_chunk at line 52-55 unchanged)

regression_test_results.test: Subagent EnterWorktree unconditionally blocked (BA Risk 4 / OBJ-3)
regression_test_results.details: agent_id='subagent-xyz' + EnterWorktree + no /do + no /allow → exit 2 with full BLOCKED message. The bypass block is gated by `if [ "$_BEW_IS_SUB" != "1" ]`; subagents fall through to the block message.

Garbage stdin → pretool-read-size-guard.py exit 0; pretool-block-enterworktree.sh exit 0 (TOOL_NAME extraction fails silently, treated as non-EnterWorktree). Matches canonical idiom of sibling hooks (pretool-write-guard.sh, pretool-bash-safety.sh).
/do flag with content 'false' → exit 2 (blocked). /do flag with empty content → exit 2 (blocked). Only exact content 'true' bypasses, mirroring pretool-orchestrator-gate.py:66-71 canonical.

git diff HEAD on all 9 files (pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, pretool-bash-safety.sh, pretool-git-privilege-guard.py, pretool-write-guard.sh, pretool-subagent-enforce.py, pretool-aggregate-check.py, prompt-workflow.py, userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh, stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh) returns no output. git status confirms none appear in modified list.

hardcode_scan_results.notes: Hook files reference standard temp-file paths (/tmp/claude-orchestrator-consent-<sid>.flag, /tmp/claude-bash-allowlist-<sid>.json). These are protocol-fixed paths shared with sibling hooks (pretool-orchestrator-gate.py, pretool-write-guard.sh) — not violations, but project conventions. No secrets, no credentials, no environment-specific URLs, no hardcoded IPs.

app_understanding_flow.skip_reason: non-UI cycle per BA spec applicability='N/A': hook + config change; cycle does not produce rendered UI changes, browser interaction, Playwright invocation, or screenshot evidence. Only PreToolUse hook scripts touched; they fire on every main-agent tool call and emit only stderr + exit codes.

e2e_enforcement.skip_justification: Non-UI cycle per BA ticket §Setup/Environment applicability='N/A': hook + config change only. The cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths (pipeline steps, API endpoints). Only PreToolUse hook scripts (pretool-read-size-guard.py, pretool-block-enterworktree.sh) are touched; they fire on every main-agent tool call and emit only stderr + exit codes. Per qa.md Step 10 'Skip condition: ONLY skip if the change affects NOTHING the user can see or trigger... hook/agent definition files.' These hook files are pure subprocess-protocol gates with stdin JSON in, stderr/exit-code out — no browser, no HTTP service, no Playwright surface to test. End-to-end verification was performed at the hook-protocol level instead: 9 ACs verified via crafted stdin JSON payloads piped to each hook binary, exit codes + stderr captured directly from process. Step 10.1 script discovery in $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR returned no *-e2e-test.py matches.

self_verification.notes: Every AC was verified by executing the hook binary against crafted stdin JSON payloads (not by reading code alone). Exit codes and stderr captured directly from process. Regression cases (subagent EnterWorktree block, bounded chunk, binary, non-Read, malformed JSON, has_consent content discipline) also empirically verified. Codex consultation was invoked and findings filtered per Calling Protocol Rule 3.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 2 findings. (1) BLOCKER: .claude/.hook-refactor-allow + .hook-refactor-allow sentinels remain on disk, disabling hook-protection invariant; codex demanded deletion before PASS/close and a synthetic Edit test exit-2. (2) MAJOR: pretool-block-enterworktree.sh regressed env-only contract — CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME=EnterWorktree + empty stdin yields exit 0 (allows non-bypass EnterWorktree); codex proposed adding env-var fallback for TOOL_NAME extraction.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 classified out_of_scope per Calling Protocol Rule 3: dispatch explicitly excludes the sentinel ('orchestrator handles cleanup post-QA, do not flag as defect'); dev pre-recorded the cleanup REQUEST in blocking_issues[0]. Top-level /.hook-refactor-allow has filesystem mtime April 27, pre-dating this cycle by ~3 weeks — not introduced here; recorded as observation for orchestrator awareness, not a cycle defect. Finding 2 classified nitpick → reject per Calling Protocol Rule 3 + Rule 4 (when in doubt, the requirement wins): independently confirmed sibling hooks pretool-write-guard.sh and pretool-bash-safety.sh exhibit identical fail-open-on-missing-stdin behavior (both exit 0 with CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME=X bash <hook> </dev/null); BA spec explicitly documented this in Risk 3 with the verbatim mitigation 'We additionally fall back fail-open on parse failure (same pattern as the other bash hooks)'; Claude Code's actual PreToolUse protocol sends JSON on stdin in production, so the empty-stdin path is a synthetic invocation that does not occur at runtime. Codex's proposed fix re-introduces the very env-only contract the BA spec deliberately moved away from to enable session_id derivation for /do and /allow bypass — accepting it would undo PATCH-2's core mechanism. All 9 ACs hold; no AC depends on env-only invocation.

codex_observation_only: Codex Finding-1: .claude/.hook-refactor-allow + .hook-refactor-allow sentinels disable hook-protection invariant. .claude/ one was created May 19 by dev (documented in dev-report blocking_issues[0]). Top-level one is pre-existing (April 27 mtime). Orchestrator should delete .claude/.hook-refactor-allow post-QA per dispatch; top-level one is a pre-existing housekeeping issue not in cycle scope.

Codex Finding-2: Env-only invocation (CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME=EnterWorktree, empty stdin) → exit 0. Identical behavior in sibling hooks pretool-write-guard.sh + pretool-bash-safety.sh (canonical fail-open-on-missing-stdin idiom). BA Risk 3 explicitly approved this mitigation. Production Claude Code PreToolUse protocol sends stdin JSON; synthetic env-only invocation does not occur in runtime.

iteration_summary: Iteration 1: BA produced ticket-20260519-151734.md (final, iter1-revised — addressed OBJ-1..OBJ-5). Dev implemented PATCH-1 (pretool-read-size-guard.py: imports + has_consent helper + bypass branch + LINE_LIMIT 1000→600) and PATCH-2 (pretool-block-enterworktree.sh: env→stdin JSON conversion + main-agent-only /do/allow bypass block, subagents still unconditionally blocked per Risk 4 / OBJ-3). Dev's self-verification confirmed all 9 ACs pass; codex consultation during dev surfaced 3 findings, 2 classified out_of_scope per BA dispatch (subagent alias coverage), 1 surfaced as blocking_issues for orchestrator (.claude/.hook-refactor-allow sentinel cleanup). QA final verification: independently re-executed all 9 ACs with crafted stdin JSON payloads — all PASS empirically. Additional empirical verifications (subagent EnterWorktree still blocked, malformed JSON fail-open, has_consent content discipline, bounded-chunk/binary/non-Read exemptions preserved, syntax compile checks, imports limited to stdlib + lib.allowlist, permissions_to_add=[]) all PASS. QA codex consultation surfaced 2 findings: (1) sentinel cleanup — explicitly out-of-scope per dispatch + dev already documented; (2) env-only invocation fail-open — matches canonical idiom of 2 sibling hooks, BA Risk 3 explicitly approved this mitigation, production runtime always sends stdin JSON. Both filtered. Verdict: PASS.
